chapter_title.ar: باب فَضْلِ ارْتِبَاطِ الْخَيْلِ وَ إِجْرَائِهَا وَ الرَّمْيِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 22 – Merits of bridling the cavalry horses and injuring them, and the throwing (Archery)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/389)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/389)
text: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ لَا سَبَقَ إِلَّا فِي خُفٍّ أَوْ حَافِرٍ أَوْ نَصْلٍ يَعْنِي النِّضَالَ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad Al Ashary, from Moala Bin Muhammad, from Al Washa, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I heard him<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘There shall be no (coming) first except in (racing) a camel-hoof, or a horse hoof, or an arrow-head, meaning archery’.
en.sarwar: 6. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad al-Ash’ariy has narrated from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Washsha’ from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “I heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘Contest is permissible only in the case of animals with Khuf (feet of camels), Hafir, (feet of horses and the like) and in the case of sharp-shooting.’”
